macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge Past $100 Amid US-Iran Conflict Escalation
items.summary: Crude oil prices broke through US$100 per barrel due to heightened Middle East tensions, including US strikes on Iran's Kharg Island and Iranian drone attacks on US bases. This has pressured global stock markets, with energy sectors rallying while growth stocks face headwinds.

items.title: Nifty and Indian Markets in Sharp Selloff, Down Over 2,000 Points Since War Start
items.summary: Nifty experienced another 2% single-day drop on March 13, with cumulative losses exceeding 2,000 points amid geopolitical tensions, FII selling, and broken technical supports. Bank Nifty fell nearly 7% last week, signaling bearish control.
items.source: YouTube (Vijay Thakkar, Neeraj Bajpai, Indraanil Guha)

items.title: Fed Rate Cut Expectations Halved as Inflation Risks Rise
items.summary: Markets slashed 2026 Fed rate cut forecasts from two to one in 48 hours following oil surge and inflation signals, with March cut probability near zero. Yellen warned of Fed caution, impacting mortgage rates above 6%.

items.title: US30 (Dow Jones) Faces Pressure from Geopolitical Tensions and War Flows
items.summary: Dow Jones analysis highlights stock market reactions to war-driven capital flows into USD, with DXY strengthening and equities declining sharply, especially in Asia. MOVE Index spike suggests deeper equity corrections ahead.

items.title: Trump Signals Further Strikes on Iran's Oil Infrastructure
items.summary: US President Trump indicated potential additional bombings on Iran's Kharg Island oil lifeline, exacerbating supply fears and contributing to crude price rally. BRICS members noted as directly involved in West Asia crisis.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades at $70,982 Amid Cautious Recovery and Stabilization Efforts
items.summary: Bitcoin is holding steady around $70,982 to $71,614, showing resilience after a correction from its 2025 peak of $126,000, with investors eyeing a potential rise above $73,000 amid macroeconomic pressures and institutional interest.[2][3][5]

items.title: Cryptocurrency Market Enters Cautious Recovery with Focus on Bitcoin and Selective Altcoins
items.summary: The broader crypto market shows constructive sentiment driven by capital inflows into ETFs, Bitcoin's macroeconomic sensitivity, and altcoin differentiation, positioning Bitcoin as the key indicator and Cardano as a mid-term pick.[1]

items.title: Galaxy Research Flags Concerns Over U.S. CLARITY Act Crypto Regulation Bill
items.summary: Alex Thorn of Galaxy Research highlighted challenges in the U.S. CLARITY Act, a proposed crypto regulation bill, amid ongoing debates on regulatory clarity.[6]

items.title: Bitcoin Flashes Rare Cycle Bottom Signal After 160-Day Correction from 2025 Highs
items.summary: A realized market cap RSI indicator suggests Bitcoin may be nearing a cycle bottom, with focus on $42,500 support, following a correction from late 2025 highs above $100,000.[3][4]

items.title: UK Plans to Deepen Crypto Ties with US, Potentially Adopting Pro-Crypto Stance
items.summary: The UK aims to strengthen cryptocurrency relations with the US and may shift toward a more pro-crypto regulatory approach, according to reports.[3]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: US Mayors Push Back Against Data Center Boom Amid Growing AI Backlash
items.summary: US mayors are resisting the rapid expansion of data centers driven by AI demands due to concerns over energy consumption and local impacts. The backlash highlights tensions between AI infrastructure growth and community interests.

items.title: New Guide Released: Measuring AI Job Automation Risk with YAML and State-of-the-Art Models
items.summary: A video tutorial demonstrates using AI to score job tasks for automation likelihood, referencing 2026 generative AI benchmarks from Artificial Analysis and Meter AI, including visualizations like Anthropic's radar charts. Updated LinkedIn algorithm insights from March 13 are also incorporated.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie's Marathon Sci-Fi Extraction Shooter Launching in March 2026
items.summary: Bungie is preparing to release Marathon, a sci-fi extraction shooter focused on PvP gameplay, high-risk encounters, and team-based survival, with a planned release in March 2026.

items.title: Clair Obscur Expedition 33 Loses Indie Game Awards GOTY Over AI Usage
items.summary: The Indie Game Awards revoked Game of the Year and Best Debut Game honors from Clair Obscur Expedition 33 after determining the game violated eligibility rules due to generative AI use during development.

items.title: Lionsgate Confirms AAA John Wick Video Game in Production
items.summary: Lionsgate has confirmed that a AAA John Wick video game is currently in development.

items.title: Video Game Industry Entering New Growth Period
items.summary: A new report indicates the video game industry is entering a new growth period.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: US Treasury Proposes 'Digital Asset Hold Law' to Freeze Crypto Without Warrants
items.summary: The US Treasury requested Congress on March 9, 2026, for powers allowing crypto exchanges to freeze digital assets instantly without warrants, expanding the Patriot Act to DeFi and enabling on-chain surveillance amid Wall Street's tokenization efforts.[2]

items.title: Light Phone 3 2026 Version Showcased at MWC 2026
items.summary: The Light Phone 3, positioned as the 'anti-Galaxy S26 Ultra,' was featured at MWC 2026, highlighting its minimalist design with a new 2026 version released recently.[3]
